directive_handled.title: Implement PROP-070 — Step A0c assigned-analyst chain-aware close sweep + Step 8 self-test + tinker observability counter

modes_checked.operator_directive: PENDING — DIRECTIVE-011 picked up and handled (preempts Mode 0-4).

backlog_trend_pre_flight.note: Backlog healthy after operator-burndown 7ec29efbbd (closed 18 stuck ISSs on 2026-05-31). PROP-070 prevents recurrence.

step_1_decider_md.change: Added Step A0c sweep (~135 lines) after PROP-058 Step A0b's closing block. Walks open-issues.json for status='assigned-analyst' entries, traverses consolidated-into-EXP chains (max depth 8, cycle-defended) across live tracker + tracker-archive, closes ISS if endpoint integrated=true with amendment-noted hold-back. Includes 48h recently-touched guard, ledger dedup via existingLedger, closure_evidence with full chain path, closure_by_mechanism='step-a0c-sweep'. Fail-loud self-test aborts commit if any remaining assigned-analyst entry has an integrated chain endpoint, >48h, not amendment-noted-held.
step_1_decider_md.anchor_used: between '**Why a sweep (mirroring PROP-056's reasoning):**' paragraph (end of A0b) and '## End-of-Run Step A: Analyst Attention Inbox'

step_2_pq_mechanics_md.change: Added Step 8 self-test (~25 lines) IMMEDIATELY AFTER Step 8 ('Close related issues — do NOT skip') and BEFORE Step 8b. Self-test reads open-issues + tracker + archive, finds the just-integrated EXP, computes leaked = exp.issue_ids.filter(id => oi.issues.find(i => i.id===id && i.status !== 'fixed' && i.status !== 'wontfix')), and FAILS LOUD + aborts commit if leaked.length > 0. Mirrors operator's fail-loud decision per PROP-070 Q-OP.

step_3_tinker_md.change: Added Mode 2 quick-check PROP-070 observability counter (~55 lines) after disk-space check, before trigger summary line. Counts assigned-analyst ISSs whose EXP-chain endpoint is integrated=true (with amendment-noted hold-back and 48h guard). Soft-complaint when >0.
step_3_tinker_md.anchor_used: after disk space check block, before 'Trigger:' line
step_3_tinker_md.followup_amendment: First post-deploy measurement found counter=3 (ISS-2311/2337/2365, all 1-10h old). Step A0c's 48h guard would correctly hold them back, so the counter was over-counting cases the sweep won't act on. Added 48h guard mirror to counter (followup commit 444898c). Post-amendment counter=0.

step_4_verification.command: grep -q 'Step A0c: assigned-analyst Chain-Aware Close Sweep' monitor/prompts/decider.md && grep -q 'Step 8 self-test (PROP-070' monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md && grep -q 'PROP-070 observability' monitor/prompts/tinker.md && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN
step_4_verification.result: FIXED (verified in both clone and FUSE post-sync)

step_8_post_deploy_canary_plan.canary_a_next_decider_run: Will verify the next decider run report includes a 'Step A0c sweep' line with migrated count + held-open count + endpoint chain paths. Pre-deploy state: 22 assigned-analyst ISSs total, 3 with integrated endpoints all <48h old (held by recency guard).
step_8_post_deploy_canary_plan.canary_b_next_tinker_mode_2: PROP-070 observability counter measured post-amendment: 0 (clean). Future Mode 2 runs should continue to read 0 unless real coverage gaps emerge.
step_8_post_deploy_canary_plan.canary_c_escalation_threshold: If next decider report omits 'Step A0c sweep' line OR if observability counter >0 for 1-2 consecutive cycles, file follow-up directive for investigation.
step_8_post_deploy_canary_plan.in_flight_observation: The 3 orphan-integrated ISSs detected pre-amendment (ISS-2311/2337/2365) point to EXPs (EXP-438/441/452) whose status=integrated=true but Step 8 close-on-integration didn't fire for them. They're all assigned-analyst with no exp_id field, EXP-NNN parsed from description. After 48h they'll become eligible for Step A0c sweep. Worth monitoring on next 2-3 decider cycles to confirm they close cleanly.

findings.title: PROP-070 observability counter initial implementation omitted 48h guard
findings.detail: Counter as initially shipped (commit ba212cf) didn't mirror Step A0c's 48h recently-touched guard, causing it to over-count fresh assigned-analyst ISSs whose chain endpoint is integrated. Confirmed on first post-deploy measurement: counter=3 for ISS-2311/2337/2365 (all 1-10h old, would be held back by the sweep's guard). Amended in followup commit 444898c. Post-amendment counter=0. Lesson: when a canary checks for residue of a sweep, its eligibility filter must match the sweep's eligibility filter or it produces spurious soft-complaints.

findings.title: 3 orphan-integrated assigned-analyst ISSs in flight (held by 48h guard)
findings.detail: ISS-2311 (EXP-438, 10h), ISS-2337 (EXP-441, 10h), ISS-2365 (EXP-452, 1h). All status='assigned-analyst', no exp_id field, EXP-NNN parsed from description. Endpoints EXP-438/441/452 are integrated=true. The 48h recently-touched guard will hold them back from auto-close for 38-47 more hours. After aging, Step A0c will sweep them. Worth verifying close-out on next 2-3 decider cycles.
findings.action: Monitor next 2-3 decider cycles; if these ISSs persist after 48h aging, escalate.

Post-deploy observability counter=0 (clean). 3 in-flight orphan-integrated cases held by 48h guard; expected to close cleanly on next 2-3 decider cycles.
